violations.suggestion: Each test should create its own test data in beforeEach for true isolation

violations.description: Tests use shared seeded user 'tecnico@hiansa.com' instead of creating test-specific data

violations.description: Tests mutate process.env.NODE_ENV directly instead of using vi.stubEnv()

recommendations: Use vi.stubEnv() instead of direct process.env mutation for auto-cleanup
Consider creating test-specific data instead of relying on shared seed data for true isolation
The current approach using seeded data is acceptable for this project size - shared seed data improves performance
beforeEach/afterEach hooks are properly implemented for state cleanup
summary: Tests are well isolated with proper beforeEach/afterEach cleanup. Only 4 minor violations found: 3 LOW (shared seed data) and 1 MEDIUM (env mutation). The use of shared seed data is a design choice that improves test performance.
